digest.top_line: DBCG IMN2: 15-yr OS 65.0% vs 60.8% (HR 0.85, p=0.0016); IMNI OS benefit confirmed in node-positive breast with modern systemic therapy, N=4541.
digest.tldr: Breast and prostate led: DBCG IMN2 locks in IMNI OS benefit at 15 yr with modern agents (HR 0.85, p=0.0016); PEACE V–STORM delivers the first RCT signal favoring ENRT over MDT for PET+ pelvic nodal oligorecurrence (HR 0.62). EORTC cutaneous lymphoma consensus formalizes low-dose RT practice without prospective trial backing.

sites.intro: Expert consensus only; no completed RCT dose-comparison data for any cutaneous lymphoma entity.

studies.name: EORTC Cutaneous Lymphoma RT Recommendations
studies.tldr: EORTC expert consensus formalizes low-dose RT recommendations across all primary cutaneous lymphoma entities; no RCT dose-comparison data exist.
details: 🔍 Expert opinion + systematic literature review; no randomized dose-comparison trials completed for any cutaneous lymphoma entity
💊 Low-dose RT endorsed for T-cell (MF, Sézary) and B-cell entities (PCMZL, PCFCL, PCDLBCL-leg type)
🔍 TSEBT for advanced-stage MF addressed; limited prospective registry data recently published
🔗 Low-dose rationale: characteristic radiosensitivity of cutaneous lymphoma lesions enables high efficacy with low toxicity and repeatable treatment
⚠️ Underlying evidence base largely retrospective case series; controlled trials defining standard dose per entity not yet completed
⚠️ Recommendations aim to reduce individualized decision-making variation until prospective trial data mature

verdict.rationale: Consensus guideline formalizing existing expert practice; no new trial data. Retrospective series only; prospective evidence gap explicitly acknowledged.

open_questions: RCT defining optimal radiation dose per cutaneous lymphoma entity
Factors influencing pt outcomes across dose levels

sites.intro: First RCT comparing ENRT vs MDT for PET-detected pelvic nodal oligorecurrence.

studies.name: PEACE V–STORM
studies.tldr: 4-yr MFS 76% (ENRT) vs 63% (MDT), HR 0.62 favoring elective nodal RT for PET+ pelvic nodal prostate oligorecurrence.
details: 🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT, N=196 randomized 1:1, 21 hospitals across 6 countries
🔍 MDT arm: SLND or SBRT 30 Gy/3 fx every other day + 6 mo ADT
🔍 ENRT arm: 45 Gy/25 fx to pelvis + SIB 65 Gy to PET+ nodes (or SLND) + 6 mo ADT
🔍 Eligibility: PET+ pelvic nodal oligorecurrence ≤5 nodes post-radical local treatment, PS 0-1
⚠️ 80% CI threshold (not 95%); p=0.063 at conventional alpha; prespecified phase 2 go/no-go design
⚠️ Open-label; heterogeneous MDT arm (SLND vs SBRT); PSMA + choline PET tracers mixed
🔗 First RCT comparing MDT vs ENRT for PET-detected pelvic nodal prostate recurrence

verdict.rationale: Phase 2 with 80% CI threshold, N=196, conventionally p=0.063; authors explicitly frame as hypothesis-generating pending phase 3.
verdict.audience: Pelvic nodal oligorecurrence (≤5 PET+ nodes), prostate, post-radical tx, PS 0-1

open_questions: Phase 3 confirmation needed before ENRT replaces MDT as SOC
ENRT benefit differential by PET tracer type (PSMA vs choline)
Optimal ADT duration in combination with ENRT

sites.intro: IMNI OS benefit durable at 15-yr f/u, confirmed in the modern taxane + trastuzumab + AI era.

studies.name: DBCG IMN2
studies.tldr: 15-yr OS 65.0% vs 60.8%, HR 0.85 (0.76-0.94), p=0.0016 favoring IMNI in node-positive breast cancer with modern systemic therapy, N=4541.
details: 📊 Primary EP OS: 15-yr 65.0% (IMNI) vs 60.8% (no IMNI)
🔍 Prospective nationwide cohort, N=4541, Jan 2007-May 2014; median f/u 13.7 yr
🔍 Allocation: right-sided → IMNI, left-sided → no IMNI; 6 RT centres, 3D-based RT
🔍 Systemic: taxane-based chemo, AIs, trastuzumab
⚠️ Cohort design, not RCT; laterality-based allocation; baseline characteristics balanced but not randomized
⚠️ No subgroup identified favoring IMNI omission, including 1-3 positive axillary nodes
🔗 vs DBCG IMN1 (N=3089, 2003-07): OS gain 4.7% at 14.8-yr f/u; confirms benefit persists in modern era
🔗 vs KROG 06-08 (negative Korean study, 3D-RT + newer agents): IMN2 contradicts null result

subdetails: Gap likely reflects left breast cardiac dose, not IMNI-specific toxicity

verdict.rationale: Large prospective cohort (not RCT) with 13.7-yr f/u confirms IMNI OS benefit persists with 3D-RT and modern systemic therapy.
verdict.audience: Node-positive breast cancer, adjuvant RT, taxane/trastuzumab/AI systemic therapy

open_questions: Benefit with post-2014 systemic agents (CDK4/6i, T-DM1) not established
Optimal IMNI technique to minimize cardiac exposure in left-sided pts
Whether cohort evidence sufficient to shift 1-3 node guidelines broadly

papers.title: Internal mammary node irradiation in 4541 node-positive breast cancer patients treated with newer systemic therapies and 3D-based radiotherapy (DBCG IMN2): a prospective, nationwide, population-based cohort study
Radiotherapy in cutaneous lymphomas: Recommendations from the EORTC cutaneous lymphoma tumour group
Salvage metastasis-directed therapy versus elective nodal radiotherapy for oligorecurrent nodal prostate cancer metastases (PEACE V–STORM): a phase 2, open-label, randomised controlled trial
